subject: Chemistry
title: Wave-Particle Duality
description: Learn Wave-Particle Duality in one minute.

hook.text: Electrons behave as particles when you detect them and as waves when you don't. They're both and neither.

buildup.text: In 1924, Louis de Broglie proposed that all matter has wave properties. The smaller the particle, the larger the wave.

discovery.text: The double-slit experiment proved it: electrons fired one at a time build up an interference pattern—like waves.

twist.text: But if you watch which slit each electron goes through, the wave pattern vanishes. Observation changes behavior.

climax.text: This duality is fundamental. Electron microscopes work because electron wavelengths are smaller than light—revealing atoms.

punchline.text: Particle or wave depends on how you look. Reality is stranger than intuition.

quiz.question: What happens when you observe which slit an electron passes through?
options: The wave interference pattern vanishes and electrons behave like particles
The electron splits into two halves
